title: Data-Center Cooling: A Drop-In Thermal Response Nonlinearity for Cross-Hall Load Classifiers
isolation: candidate runs as an isolated stdin->stdout subprocess via isorun; sensor data, labels, trained weights and oracle state stay in the evaluator process
strategies: identity curve phi(x)=x -> reproduces the evaluator's linear baseline (~0.1 on every hall)
plain unit-gain tanh -> real curvature, beats linear on several halls but under-uses the knot range and saturates early
gain-tuned saturating unit 2*tanh(1.5x) -> steep live gradient near 0 + wide bounded plateau; separates the nonlinear halls while staying stable on the near-linear blobs hall -> high geometric mean
rectifier-family curves (relu/gelu/swish/elu) -> excellent on XOR/rings but collapse the near-linear Gaussian-regime hall below baseline, zeroing the geometric mean
even/radial curves (abs) -> strong on rings/ring4d but poor on spirals/moons
per-setting adaptation using the public 'setting'/'n_features'/'n_classes' fields to pick a curve shape per hall (candidate never sees the labelled data, so it cannot overfit)
